title: tragiella anomala
intro: Tragiella anomala, commonly known as the "Asiatic pennywort" or "Guduchi", is a type of plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The therapeutic actions of Tragiella anomala are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including withanolides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Additionally, Tragiella anomala has been reported to have a potential role in managing stress, anxiety, and other mental health conditions due to its adaptogenic properties.
side_effects: Tragiella anomala can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also lead to respiratory problems, including bronchitis and asthma-like symptoms. In rare cases, improper use of the plant may cause liver and kidney damage due to its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Additionally, using Tragiella anomala for medicinal purposes without proper guidance can result in interactions with other medications, leading to adverse effects.
